facts.statement: Session opening — no issue domain identified
No party input captured — cannot assess feasibility of any option structure
Business valuation complexity — Party A has signaled this explicitly; assessment cannot proceed without understanding structure, Party B's role, and valuation approach
Three asset categories identified (home, accounts, business) — but relative value, liquidity, and party preferences completely unknown
CONFIRMED: Parties are negotiating disclosure protocol (raw documents vs. professional intermediation, immediate vs. staged) — this is a process design dispute, not yet an asset division dispute
CONFIRMED: No agreement on business valuation approach — Party A proposes joint professional, Party B requests raw documents first, no convergence
CRITICAL: Financial disclosure is a non-negotiable foundational requirement in divorce mediation — not a timing preference or negotiable detail
CRITICAL: Party A's deflection pattern (three turns, three justifications) constitutes active refusal of disclosure obligation despite direct request

facts_notes: Facts remain separated from unresolved feasibility questions.
